mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding household income and consumption demand, which drives higher expected corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the sign of predicted direction matches actual direction in fewer than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or greater rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which crushes consumer spending forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return during trigger periods is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: While the federal funds rate is anchored below 0.25%, a concurrent three-month rise in consumer prices erodes real short-term yields, forcing capital out of cash instruments and into equities, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL rises over prior 3 months
failure_condition: Joint signal instances fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual series in a linear model of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month gains in industrial production signal inventory restocking and rising operating leverage among manufacturers, which lifts aggregate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is negative or not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous three-month increases in retail sales and housing starts reveal a broad-based demand recovery spanning consumption and residential investment, which amplifies cyclical earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or a portfolio sorted on the interaction underperforms a linear combination of the two single-series signals by more than 200 basis points annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation falling below 1% signals deflationary pressure that raises real debt burdens and squeezes corporate pricing power, which depresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During months when the trigger is active, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return during triggered months exceeds the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly real GDP print following two consecutive quarters of contraction marks the end of recession, triggering a repricing of earnings growth from depressed bases and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly change > 0 after two consecutive negative quarterly changes
failure_condition: Triggered quarterly observations fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the average 60-day return following the trigger is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly increases in housing starts signal a durable turnaround in residential investment and construction employment, which sparks a sharp repricing of cyclical exposures and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following three consecutive monthly increases in HOUST, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the hit rate for positive returns falls below 45%.
